source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/11_Audit F-up/240415-17 ISO 갱신심사 & 2,3G MDR 심사 - 3EC/MDR 보완/[2nd response TD by H&abyz] Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector (08 March 2024) pdf/A-TCF-5-155 Risk Management/A-TCF-5-103 Risk Management Plan (rev.3).pdf

document.title: Risk Management Plan for A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

sourceSummary.oneLine: H&abyz risk management plan for A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detectors under Rev. 3 dated March 8, 2024.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Establishes and documents the risk management plan, including scope, responsibilities, risk acceptability criteria, residual risk evaluation, verification activities, and production/post-production information review.
keyPoints: The plan applies to all stages of the life cycle of A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW.
The product is classified as IIb and has an expected service life of approximately 5 years.
Risk level is calculated as Severity rating × Probability rating.
Risk levels 1~4 are Green, Acceptable Risk; risk levels 5~25 are Red, Unacceptable Risk.

Post-production information is collected from internal sources such as PMCF, CAPA reports, customer complaints, service records, and satisfaction surveys, and external sources such as recalls, side effects reports, literature, similar device information, and legal or standards trends.

concepts.name: Regulation (EU) 2017/745
concepts.evidence: Regulation (EU) 2017/745 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 5 April 2017

evidence.claim: The document is Technical Documentation Document No. A-TCF-5-103, Risk Management Plan, Rev. No. 3.
evidence.evidenceText: Technical Documentation Document No. A-TCF-5-103 Rev. No. 3 Risk Management Plan Rev. Date March 8, 2024

evidence.claim: The document date is March 8, 2024.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk Management Plan Rev. Date March 8, 2024

evidence.claim: The document covers A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk Management Plan for A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: Revision 3 was a response to the 2nd review by 3EC (NB).
evidence.evidenceText: 3 Mar/8/2024 Response to 2nd review by 3EC (NB)

evidence.claim: The plan includes scope, responsibilities, review requirements, risk acceptability criteria, residual risk evaluation, verification activities, and production/post-production information activities.
evidence.evidenceText: This plan includes the following: a) the scope of the planned risk management activities... b) assignment of responsibilities and authorities; c) requirements for review of risk management activities; d) criteria for risk acceptability... e) a method to evaluate the overall residual risk... f) activities for verification... and g) activities related to collection and review of relevant production and post-production information.

evidence.claim: The applicable national and regional regulation listed is Regulation (EU) 2017/745 for the European Union.
evidence.evidenceText: European Union Regulation (EU) 2017/745 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 5 April 2017

evidence.claim: The product is classified as IIb.
evidence.evidenceText: Product Name Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Model Name A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW Classification IIb

evidence.claim: The expected service life is approximately 5 years.
evidence.evidenceText: Expected service life Approximately 5 years

evidence.claim: The intended purpose is a digital imaging solution for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting adults and children.
evidence.evidenceText: The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector intended purpose

evidence.claim: The devices are intended to replace film based radiographic diagnostic systems and support real-time diagnosis and treatment planning.
evidence.evidenceText: It is intended to replace film based radiographic diagnostic systems and provide more precise case diagnosis and treatment planning on a real time basis for physicians and radiologists.

evidence.claim: The plan applies to all stages of the product life cycle.
evidence.evidenceText: This Risk management plan is applicable to all stages of the life cycle of the A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW.

evidence.claim: The detector is described as a wired or wireless digital flat panel detector designed for digital radiography systems.
evidence.evidenceText: The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW are wired or wireless digital flat panel detectors that have been designed for faster, more streamlined approach to digital radiography systems.

evidence.claim: The detector consists of TFT, CsI scintillator, and electronics converting data into digital signals.
evidence.evidenceText: The detector consists of TFT and Scintillator (CsI) and an electronic device that converts data read through it into digital signals.

evidence.claim: The detectors support auto exposure detection without generator integration.
evidence.evidenceText: The detectors support an auto exposure detection technology that allows the detectors to be used without generator integration.

evidence.claim: Risk management responsibilities are assigned to R&D Manager Lee, Dong jin; R&D Engineer Kim, Kihyun; and QM Representative Hur Namkyu.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk management final approved QM Representative Hur Namkyu

evidence.claim: Risk acceptability criteria are based on applicable regulations, international standards, state of the art, and stakeholder concerns.
evidence.evidenceText: This policy shall ensure that criteria are based upon applicable national or regional regulations and relevant International Standards, and take into account available information such as the generally accepted state of the art and known stakeholder concerns.

evidence.claim: Risk probability is rated from 1 Improbable to 5 Frequent.
evidence.evidenceText: Step Probability Definition 5 Frequent More than 1 time / day ... 1 Improbable Less than 1 time / every other year (2 years) or hardly occur

evidence.claim: Risk severity is rated from 1 Negligible to 5 Catastrophic.
evidence.evidenceText: Severity levels Definition 5 Catastrophic Death of patient, irreversible failure or disaster ... 1 Negligible Discomfort, temporary difficulty

evidence.claim: Risk level is calculated as severity rating multiplied by probability rating.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk level = Severity rating × Probability rating

evidence.claim: Risk levels 1 to 4 are acceptable and risk levels 5 to 25 are unacceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk Level: 1~4 Green, Acceptable Risk (A) Acceptable region Risk Level: 5~25 Red, Unacceptable Risk (U) Unacceptable region

evidence.claim: Unacceptable risk policy is project cancellation or device redesign.
evidence.evidenceText: Unacceptable Risk (U) Unacceptable risk Project cancellation or device redesign

evidence.claim: Overall residual risk is evaluated after risk control measures have been implemented and verified.
evidence.evidenceText: After all risk control measures have been implemented and verified, the manufacturer shall evaluate the overall residual risk posed by the medical device

evidence.claim: If overall residual risk is acceptable, users are informed of significant residual risks through accompanying documentation.
evidence.evidenceText: If the overall residual risk is judged acceptable, the manufacturer shall inform users of significant residual risks and shall include the necessary information in the accompanying documentation in order to disclose those residual risks.

evidence.claim: The plan compares the devices with similar medical devices available on the market when evaluating overall residual risk.
evidence.evidenceText: The H&abyz Co., Ltd. compares the A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW under consideration to similar medical devices available on the market.

evidence.claim: Risk management verification activities are performed according to product life cycle phases.
evidence.evidenceText: The risk management verification activities shall be performed in accordance with the product life cycle phases.

evidence.claim: Post-production information is collected from internal and external sources.
evidence.evidenceText: The get information method of post-production information should be collected according to the internal information and the external information.

evidence.claim: The Quality manager performs risk analysis for collected information once every 12 months.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk analysis for the collected information (once every 12 months)

evidence.claim: The Quality manager reports safety information and side effects to the regulatory agency if necessary.
evidence.evidenceText: According to the seriousness of the information collected, the Quality manager shall report safety information and any side effects to the regulatory agency (Applicable national or regional regulations).

reviewFlags.reason: Metadata authoritySignal is FDA, but the source text does not mention FDA; authority evidence in the source supports European Union Regulation (EU) 2017/745, 3EC (NB), and Korean medical device references.
The source text contains OCR or formatting artifacts and wording errors, but key document metadata and risk management facts are readable.
